Janet Fordham died in crash after travelling to see man who claimed he would help to recover money from earlier scams

A British woman who was scammed of up to £1m in a string of so-called romance frauds died in a road crash after travelling to west Africa to try to recoup some of her lost fortune, an inquest in Devon has heard.

Janet Fordham was cheated of her life savings and her home over a period of five years by fraudsters apparently based in the UK, Germany, the US and Ghana, the inquest in Exeter was told.

Research shows natural hazards linked to climate crisis disrupted 23 elections in 18 countries in 2024

Democracy is under mounting threat from the climate crisis, with new analysis documenting how elections are increasingly shaped not only by political forces but also by floods, wildfires and extreme weather.

At least 94 elections and referendums across 52 countries have been disrupted by climate-related impacts over the last two decades, researchers found.

The resettlement talks, first reported by the New York Times, come after Donald Trump’s decision to stop an initiative that allowed Afghans who assisted US war efforts to apply to resettle in the US.

Actor and former ballet dancer Charlize Theron has joined the chorus of disapproval aimed at Timothée Chalamet over his remarks that appeared to disrespect performers of ballet and opera.

In an interview with the New York Times, Theron said: “Oh, boy, I hope I run into him one day,” adding: “That was a very reckless comment on two art forms that we need to lift up constantly because, yes, they do have a hard time. But in 10 years, AI is going to be able to do Timothée’s job, but it will not be able to replace a person on a stage dancing live.”

Prosecutors say the charges against alleged members of the Mara Salvatrucha gang, or MS-13, span more than 47,000 crimes committed between 2012 and 2022, including a weekend that was El Salvador’s bloodiest since its civil war.

The accident happened early on Sunday, as the officials were driving back from the scene of the raid. Their vehicle skidded off the road and plunged down a 200 metre ravine in the mountains near Chihuahua’s border with the state of Sinaloa.

By 4pm, the light was softening over the Pacific, and the crew of the Don Maca were finishing a long day hauling in lines of swordfish and albacore. Down in the hold, the mood had settled into the familiar rhythm of a fishing day drawing to a close.

The shooting – the latest violent incident to affect Mexico as it prepares to co-host the football World Cup in June – took place on Monday lunchtime and was captured in mobile phone videos.

The world’s top condom producer, Malaysia’s Karex Bhd, plans to raise prices by 20% to 30% and possibly further if supply chain disruptions drag on due to the Iran war, its chief executive has said.

Karex is also seeing a surge in condom demand as rising freight costs and shipping delays have left many of its customers with lower stockpiles than usual, CEO Goh Miah Kiat told Reuters in an interview on Tuesday.

The student, who the Guardian has chosen not to name, lost contact with his friends in Sydney after returning to China in December 2024.

Ariyoshi, a Democrat who led the state from 1973 to 1986, died peacefully while surrounded by family on Sunday night, according to a statement Monday from the current governor, Josh Green.

The Australian mining company Santana Minerals is pushing to expedite a 85-hectare (210-acre) open-cast goldmine, called Bendigo-Ophir, in the Dunstan mountains, an area described as “outstanding natural landscape” by the Central Otago district council.

Two state governments have drastically underdelivered more than $160m in infrastructure measures to improve river health in the northern Murray-Darling basin eight years since they were promised, a major independent review has found.

events.title: How Ireland’s war-driven fuel blockades revealed the true cost of Europe’s oil addiction
events.summary:
Donald Trump’s conflict with Iran could speed the EU’s green revolution – if panicking governments can hold their nerve on clean energy

• Don’t get This Is Europe delivered to your inbox? Sign up here

A surge in demand for electric vehicles across Europe may be evidence of what George Monbiot greeted as the silver lining of the Iran war. Sales of electric cars in continental Europe rose by 51% in March.

The International Energy Agency has called the disruption in the strait of Hormuz the “biggest energy crisis in history”, but it appears, on one level, to be accelerating Europe’s green revolution. Yet, even if car-owners are rushing to the EV showrooms, some European governments, facing a groundswell of anger over soaring petrol and gas prices, are at risk of sending the clean energy transition into reverse.

If you’re just joining us, here’s the main news of the day. It is 9.30am in Tehran, 9am in Jerusalem and Beirut, and 2am in Washington DC.

Donald Trump unilaterally said he is extending the ceasefire with Iran at Pakistan’s request while awaiting a “unified proposal” from Tehran, even as the US military maintains its blockade of Iranian ports.

Trump made the announcement as ceasefire talks looked increasingly uncertain with a two-week truce set to expire on Wednesday. Both countries had said they were prepared to resume fighting if no deal is reached.

Trump said he would “extend the ceasefire until such time as [Iran’s] proposal is submitted, and discussions are concluded, one way or the other”.

Trump later claimed in a Truth Social post that Iran is “collapsing financially” and was losing $500m every day that the strait of Hormuz is effectively closed.

Iran has yet to decide whether to join the negotiations in Pakistan, a foreign ministry spokesman said earlier on Tuesday, and will only take part if Tehran believes the discussions would yield results.

A container ship has reported being fired at by an IRGC gunboat, the United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations said. The incident occurred 15 nautical miles north-east of Oman. The vessel sustained “heavy damage” to its bridge, the master of the ship said. All crew members were reported as safe.

Shares were mixed in Asia as markets waited to see if the US and Iran may resume talks. Brent crude edged higher to $98.51 a barrel, while US benchmark crude fell 0.4% to $89.29 a barrel.

One person was killed and two others wounded in an Israeli drone strike overnight on the outskirts of al-Jbour in Lebanon’s western Bekaa Valley, Lebanese state media reported. Israel and Lebanon agreed to a 10-day ceasefire on Friday.

Since the war started, fighting has killed at least 3,375 people in Iran and more than 2,290 in Lebanon, the Associated Press reported. Additionally, 23 people have died in Israel and more than a dozen in Gulf Arab states. Fifteen Israeli soldiers in Lebanon and 13 US service members throughout the region have been killed.

Iran’s Revolutionary Guards threatened to prevent oil production in the Middle East if the Islamic republic faced attacks launched from its Gulf neighbours’ territory.

Israel’s observer status at the Council of Europe’s parliamentary assembly could be suspended over the country’s new law mandating the death penalty for Palestinians convicted of some offences, the president of the body has said.

Pakistani officials, mediating between an unpredictable US president and hardliners in Tehran, were on Monday trying to coax both sides to put the conditions in place for a second round of talks in Islamabad this week, including easing the standoff in the strait of Hormuz. UK inflation accelerated to 3.3% in March after the Iran war triggered the biggest jump in fuel prices for more than three years, Richard Partington reports.

Today the Liberal Democrats staged a photocall to publicise their line about this being “Trumpflation”. Daisy Cooper, the Lib Dem deputy leader and Treasury spokesperson, said:

People across our country have been struggling for years with a devastating cost-of-living crisis and Donald Trump’s idiotic war in Iran has added to it. The cost of fuel is soaring, mortgage rates are rising and fixed energy deals are already going up by hundreds of pounds.

But what is utterly inexcusable is that there are politicians in this country - Nigel Farage and Kemi Badenoch - who are happy to cheerlead Donald Trump as he hikes people’s bills. All the while this Labour government promised to fix the country but instead we’ve got political Groundhog Day: yet more sleaze and scandal.

A federal judge in Massachusetts on Tuesday struck down several Trump administration actions slowing down development of clean energy, including a requirement that all solar and wind energy projects on federal lands and waters be personally approved by the interior secretary, Doug Burgum.

events.summary: A 12-year-old was among those killed as Russian missile and drone attacks hit Ukrainian cities of Kyiv, Odesa and Dnipro.

events.title: Senegal: The Goethe-Institut opens its new building in Dakar
events.summary: The Goethe-Institut's new building in Dakar champions sustainable architecture, made from the earth. Star architect Francis Kere has blended local materials with contemporary design.

events.title: Anatomy of the clitoris: 3D images reveal its nerve system
events.summary: New 3D images reveal the clitoris’s complex nerve network, highlighting long‑standing gaps in medical understanding of the female body.

events.title: Top EU court rules online gamblers can sue for compensation if betting illegal in home country
events.summary: The ECJ has ruled that online gamblers can seek compensation if they lost money when gambling was illegal in their home country, even if the laws have since changed. This could pave the way for others to reclaim losses.

events.title: Germany-Israel divide deepens after Merz criticism
events.summary: The Israeli finance minister's sharp criticism of the German chancellor's views on Israel's settlement policy is just the latest sign of a growing estrangement. What's the current state of German-Israeli relations?

events.title: US jury finds Live Nation, Ticketmaster hold illegal monopoly
events.summary: The verdict will not bring immediate relief to concertgoers frustrated by high ticket prices, but could cost the company hundreds of millions of dollars.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Fans with disabilities 'excluded'
events.summary: Complaints that the 2026 World Cup, hosted largely by the USA, is a rip off have been widespread and constant. But it's not just the prices that are making it feel impossible for football fans with disabilities.

events.title: Germany news: Teacher group urges action over pupil violence
events.summary: A German teachers' group says decisive action is needed to counter a surge in violence in classrooms and corridors. Meanwhile, the Greens say a blanket speed limit on major highways would help save fuel.

events.title: UK signs deal to rejoin EU's Erasmus exchange scheme
events.summary: Britain will rejoin the popular Europe-wide student exchange program six years after leaving it.

events.title: Berlin hosts Sudan conference amid brutal, forgotten war
events.summary: The war in Sudan has been raging for the past three years, and a ceasefire is nowhere in sight. Participants at a conference of donors in Berlin hope at least to ease the suffering of the people caught in the middle.

events.title: German health minister announces billions in cutbacks
events.summary: The comprehensive reform package aims to plug a multibillion-euro hole in Germany's expensive health care system. Reforms include mandatory second opinions for costly surgeries and no more coverage for homeopathy.

events.title: Solar power in Morocco's desert: Bold vision, mixed results
events.summary: A massive solar tower in the Moroccan desert is the beacon of an ambitious push for a clean energy future. But fossil fuels and grid constraints stand in the way.

events.title: Women's workwear: When proper fit becomes a safety issue
events.summary: With limited options, many women in trades and industry have to wear men's workwear. But a poor fit causes discomfort and can compromise safety. Now, some firms are stepping up to design safer, better-fitting gear.

events.title: What we call sex work — and what it says about society
events.summary: A Bonn exhibition traces the cultural history of sex work and shows how the words used to describe it have shaped stigma, policy and lived experience.

events.title: Germany: Man found guilty in Pelicot-style rape case
events.summary: A Chinese student has been found guilty of drugging and raping a woman in Munich over a period of months. The perpetrator was a member of a Telegram chat group where men organized rapes and shared images of their crimes.

events.title: Lufthansa pilots to strike again for 2 more days, leading to a full working week of disruption
events.summary: Pilots at Germany's Lufthansa airline will strike again on Thursday and Friday, the Vereinigung Cockpit trade union said. Cabin crew are also striking this week, clouding the company's centenary celebrations

events.title: Sudan: Iran war prompts 'massive' food, fuel price rises; drones kill hundreds of civilians in 2026
events.summary: German charities warn of increases of 70-80% in the costs of essentials in wartorn Sudan amid the unrest in the Middle East. Donors gather in Berlin on Wednesday on the third anniversary of the outbreak of the war.

events.title: Germany's aid to Ukraine faces challenges
events.summary: At Ukrainian-German government talks, Berlin reassures Kyiv it can continue counting on military, civilian, and humanitarian aid, even as political opposition and US cuts create challenges for sustained support.

events.title: Germany debates rape law to tackle AI and online abuse
events.summary: Calls are growing for Germany to update its rape law after a celebrity case involving "deepfakes" caused an uproar. Campaigners are also pushing for a move to the "only yes means yes" principle of active consent.

events.title: The world ditched wasteful toilets, the US stayed behind
events.summary: US toilets use far more water than many of their global counterparts. President Donald Trump is pushing to loosen water pressure standards, a move critics say would increase waste.

events.title: Lufthansa pilots launch 2-day strike, cabin crew say they will strike right after
events.summary: Pilots who are members of the Vereinigung Cockpit trade union are striking on Monday and Tuesday. As hundreds of flights were canceled, the UFO cabin crew union said it would also strike on Wednesday and Thursday.

events.title: Trump's Strait of Hormuz blockade hits Iran's oil trade
events.summary: A US naval blockade cuts off Iran's main source of hard currency by halting most of its oil exports. Will President Trump's pressure force Tehran back to negotiations or widen the conflict?

events.title: Germany's Buchenwald: Remembering Nazi atrocities
events.summary: Buchenwald was one of the largest concentration camps on German soil. Eighty‑one years ago, it was liberated. The commemoration included moving words — and protests.

events.title: World's oldest gorilla celebrates 69th birthday at Berlin Zoo
events.summary: Lady Fatou, known as the "grand dame" of the Berlin Zoo, was certified last year by Guinness as the oldest living gorilla in the world.

events.title: Germany news: Merz says fuel tax lowered to ease price surge
events.summary: Berlin is cutting fuel tax and arms manufacturer Rheinmetall is preparing to build missiles in a new venture. Elsewhere, a fisherman triggered a massive search before being found drunk and asleep in a car. DW has more.

events.title: Merck's Keytruda: A lifesaving drug, a global divide
events.summary: A cross-border investigation by DW and ICIJ reveals how pricing and patents helped turn a life-saving medicine into one of the world's best selling drugs while leaving many patients struggling to access it.

events.title: Germany: Betting on elections is illegal — but possible
events.summary: Online gambling is gaining traction in Germany. Using prediction markets like Polymarket to bet on political events remains illegal, but it is almost impossible to police.

events.title: UFOs: 'Most are reported by people out for an evening smoke'
events.summary: Hansjürgen Köhler has been a volunteer UFO hunter for more than 50 years. He sees his work as scientific investigation, but sometimes also as caring for others.

events.title: First gay rights movement: Berlin's wild 1920s queer history
events.summary: Before the Nazis took power, Berlin was a center of LGBTQ+ rights and queer culture, with research institutes, a flourishing nightlife and one of the earliest gay rights' movements.

events.title: NASA's billion-dollar space race goes into overdrive
events.summary: NASA's renewed push to get a human mission to the moon and Mars is neither cheap, nor for the faint of heart. But who ends up covering the costs and what does everyone still on Earth get out of all this?

events.title: Recycling's next big thing — or big bluff?
events.summary: An advanced recycling process claims it can handle hard-to-recycle plastics, like packaging. But critics say it's not worth the effort.

events.title: Has Coachella festival sold out?
events.summary: One of the world's biggest open-air music and art festivals has been hit by controversies and declining credibility — yet the world's biggest acts continue to headline.

events.title: Veteran German actor Mario Adorf dies aged 95
events.summary: Few actors had a career as rich as Mario Adorf. He played legendary Hollywood villains, wrote German TV history and still had film roles at age 90.

events.title: Artemis II crew emotional as mission prepares for reentry
events.summary: In less than 48 hours, the Orion spacecraft is due to splash down in the Pacific Ocean. Crew members have said what they saw in space will forever change how they see the world.

events.title: Hungary election: How football has helped Orban keep power
events.summary: Viktor Orban is a football fanatic but has long used the sport as a political tool, both in Hungary and abroad. With polls predicting election defeat, he could also lose his chance of hosting the Champions League final.

events.title: Norway's Telenor faces lawsuit for giving junta data from its customers in Myanmar
events.summary: A Swedish non-profit has filed a class action lawsuit against Norway's Telenor, accusing it of endangering customers in Myanmar by sharing their data with the junta. The company said it had no real choice.

events.title: How farmers are rising to a fertilizer shortfall
events.summary: The war in Iran has sparked a global fertilizer shortage and is threatening food security. What are farmers around the world doing to save their crops?

events.title: Should Western influencers promote Taliban‑run Afghanistan?
events.summary: In the race for clicks, likes and followers, some travel content creators are being criticized for promoting Afghanistan while ignoring the harsh reality of Taliban rule.

events.title: 60 years after the Indonesian mass killings: Is the Cold War back?
events.summary: Six decades after helping the Indonesian army crush one of the biggest communist parties in the world, the US is once again meddling in countries it considers a thorn in its flesh. Is history repeating itself?

events.title: Will AI doom office space to history?
events.summary: Artificial intelligence's potential for disruption is being felt across multiple industries. Now commercial real estate is feeling the heat from what analysts say is a "paradigm shift."

events.title: Homegrown energy is slashing US energy bills
events.summary: In times of skyrocketing energy prices, some US citizens are making big savings by producing electricity at home.

events.title: One in five young Germans plan to leave the country
events.summary: A recent study shows that as many as one in five Germans between the ages of 14 and 29 are actively thinking about moving to another country — and 40% plan to do so in the long term.

events.title: Can small nuclear reactors solve Europe's energy woes?
events.summary: Supporters say modular reactors are key to meeting energy goals, but critics say they're a costly distraction.

events.title: Mediterranean sharks are vanishing in a legal void
events.summary: Along Libya's coast, sharks — including breeding females — are being sold unchecked. With little enforcement and growing financial pressure on fishermen, vulnerable species are spiraling toward extinction.

events.title: 'Drill baby drill' ― Trump opens up nature to big energy
events.summary: America's beloved national parks and public lands face conservation rollbacks and sell-offs as the administration of US President Donald Trump pushes for fossil fuel and timber extraction.

events.title: Semenya hits out at impact of Olympic ban on DSD athletes
events.summary: Caster Semenya has had her gender scrutinized in public for years after winning two Olympic golds. She has hit out at what she sees as a policy that disproportionately affects athletes with sexual differences.

events.title: Wealth tax: Why are countries afraid to tax the ultrarich?
events.summary: Growing income inequality and public displays of billionaire power are increasing calls for new income and wealth taxes aimed at the world's richest individuals. Can wealth ever be taxed fairly?

events.title: How US 'Liberation Day' tariffs reshaped global trade
events.summary: One year after Trump's "Liberation Day" tariffs, global trade flows have shifted. Trade data shows which countries gained, which lost — and who is footing the bill.

events.title: UNESCO World Heritage sites facing the heat
events.summary: From Easter Island to the Great Wall of China, many of the world's most iconic cultural heritage sites are under threat from rising temperatures linked to climate change.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Iraq qualify but Iran doubts loom
events.summary: Iraq have overcome tough circumstances to reach the World Cup for the first time in 30 years, with the lineup now complete. But Iran's potential withdrawal still hangs over a tournament fraught with political issues.

events.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Trump's politics put off German fans
events.summary: Many German fans are torn on whether to travel to the US for this summer's major football tournament.

events.title: Tiger Woods 'stepping away' for treatment after DUI arrest
events.summary: Tiger Woods has announced a break from golf four days after he crashed his SUV and was arrested for driving under influence. Fans had been awaiting his return at the Masters this month.

events.title: Were Grandma and Grandpa Nazis?
events.summary: What did our forebears do during the Nazi era? A subject long shrouded in silence in German families. Now, the US National Archives has made millions of NSDAP membership cards available online.

events.title: FIFA World Cup: Which German players will make the final cut?
events.summary: Several players on Germany's national football team have really stood out during the squad's recent test matches ahead of this summer's major soccer tournament.

events.title: After the Iran war, how fast could global trade recover?
events.summary: The Iran war could end soon as a ceasefire goes into effect. But the true test will be how quickly energy, fertilizer and other supply chains can bounce back whenever the Strait of Hormuz is reopened.

events.title: Germany beat Ghana to complete confidence-boosting March
events.summary: A late winner from Stuttgart's Deniz Undav secured a second friendly win in a week for Germany. With the FIFA World Cup just 10 weeks away, Julian Nagelsmann's team will be starting to believe they can win it all.

events.title: Air Canada CEO to retire after English-only condolence furor
events.summary: Air Canada has announced that CEO Michael Rousseau will retire later this year. This follows public criticism of his failure to voice condolences in French as well as English after two pilots were killed in a collision.

events.title: The pleasure of books in the digital age
events.summary: The digitization of global literature has brought vast libraries to our fingertips. But the quiet power of a physical book offers a rare escape from constant digital noise.

events.title: Amnesty flags FIFA World Cup human rights risks
events.summary: The human rights group has sounded the alarm about risks faced by visitors to the 2026 FIFA World Cup. It goes as far as to describe the situation in the United States as a "human rights emergency."

events.title: Solar is winning the energy race
events.summary: The world's cheapest power source is scaling at warp speed, pushing coal, gas and nuclear aside.

events.title: At 40, Lady Gaga isn't done revolutionizing pop music
events.summary: As iconic as she is outlandish, Lady Gaga has left quite a mark on the world of music. Here's how she reinvented pop — and reached millions by opening up about her trauma and illness.

events.title: Florian Wirtz inspires Germany past Switzerland
events.summary: Germany beat Switzerland 4-3 in an entertaining World Cup warm-up in Basel. Two goals and two assists from Liverpool's Florian Wirtz secured a confidence-boosting victory for Julian Nagelsmann's team.

events.title: Daylight saving time: A history of fact, folklore and fuss
events.summary: From wartime strategy to Halloween lobbying lore, the legacy of daylight saving time is tangled in culture, commerce and contested claims.

events.title: Fighting in Iran: Is another 1970s‑style oil crisis looming?
events.summary: Supply disruptions, high prices, nervous markets: The current war in the Middle East brings back memories of the oil crises of the 1970s. Back then, many economies plunged into stagflation. How bad is the current crisis?

events.title: 'Looksmaxxing' — the manosphere beauty cult
events.summary: The online trend with roots in misogyny encourages socially isolated young men to undertake painful practices to gain beauty and status — and its language is reaching the White House.

events.title: Smart sleepers could be the glow-up night trains need
events.summary: Europe has a bit of an on-off relationship with night trains. A Berlin team's smart cabin concept could lead to greater commitment.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Cost, Trump and travel give fans pause
events.summary: As teams battle it out for the final six spots, loyal fans are making plans for World Cup 2026. DW spoke to supporters across the world to hear about five-figure expenditure, visa uncertainty and safety concerns.

events.title: Iran war boosts strategic significance of South Caucasus
events.summary: With war raging in Iran, the South Caucasus has become one of the few stable corridors between Asia and Europe. Nowhere are the conflict's opportunities and risks more visible than in Azerbaijan.

events.title: Germany's green reputation hits a crossroads
events.summary: Germany's new climate spend is big on wind power and e‑mobility. Some call it real momentum, others say it’s basically a green band‑aid.

events.title: Is VW eyeing Iron Dome parts production at German plant?
events.summary: A report in the Financial Times says Volkswagen is considering producing defense components for Israel. A VW spokesperson told DW the company was exploring "viable options."

events.title: Is Iran cashing in millions from Strait of Hormuz blockade?
events.summary: An Iranian lawmaker claims Tehran is quietly charging tankers up to $2 million for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz. The move threatens to turn the key waterway into a high-stakes toll booth.

events.title: From Goethe to Soraya: German-Iranian stories
events.summary: It's hard to imagine now, but Iran and Germany were once surprisingly close. A master poet, a German "Lawrence of Arabia," and an empress all played a role in this shared history.

events.title: WNBA's labor deal is a structural shift for women's sports
events.summary: America's top women's basketball league just agreed to a new CBA. It will have a significant impact on women's sport.

events.title: NASA plans moon base instead of orbital lunar station
events.summary: New NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman says the US space agency will pivot from launching an orbiting lunar station to constructing a base on the moon's surface. It comes amid a renewed moon race with Russia and China.

events.title: American apocalypse: The end 'feels personal and imminent'
events.summary: Social psychologists in the US found that a third of people surveyed feel the world will end in their lifetimes. That's not only depressing; it also affects people's sense of agency when faced by war or climate change.

events.title: Iran seizes ships in Strait of Hormuz after Trump halts attacks
events.summary: Iran seized two ships in the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday, April 22, tightening its grip on the strategic route.

events.title: Mapping the environmental toll of the Middle East war
events.summary: Oil refineries set ablaze, missiles hitting pesticide factories, an oil slick in the Strait of Hormuz... In under two months, the US and Israel’s war on Iran has inflicted a heavy toll on the environment, fuelling concerns over the public health impact on local populations.

events.title: From pop royalty to raw Americana: 'Michael', 'The New West' and 'Die, My Love'
events.summary: This week's film show on arts24 spotlights a trio of strikingly different releases, led by Antoine Fuqua's highly anticipated biopic "Michael", which features a breakout performance from nephew Jaafar Jackson as the King of Pop, alongside Colman Domingo's chilling turn as patriarch Joseph Jackson.

events.title: Prize-winning book 'Houris' brings prison term for French-Algerian author Kamel Daoud
events.summary: French-Algerian author Kamel Daoud says he has been sentenced in Algeria to three years in prison and fined over his award-winning novel “Houris,” which revisits the country’s civil war. The ruling reflects the mounting pressure on authors confronting Algeria’s violent past and raises fresh concerns over freedom of expression.

events.title: Food security: Why Guterres’s UN plan to get fertiliser flowing in Hormuz is stalling
events.summary: The United Nations is pushing its proposal for a humanitarian corridor to get fertiliser and other essential goods through the Strait of Hormuz, which has been effectively blocked since the start of the Iran war. This plan, which FRANCE 24 has seen, has yet to be approved by several countries.

events.title: Unblocked EU loan provides relief to Ukraine's strained finances
events.summary: European Union ambassadors unblocked ⁠on Wednesday a ​90-billion-euro ($106 billion) loan to Ukraine that is critical for Kyiv's ability to keep up the fight against Russia. The loan, which ​was approved by the European Council in December, had been halted by a Hungarian veto amid a dispute over oil flows via the Druzhba pipeline.

events.title: France boosts fuel subsidies amid mounting cost-of-living pressure
events.summary: As the French government expands its support for rising energy costs, Prime Minister Lecornu has announced a significant increase in monthly fuel subsidies aimed at helping low-income workers and key industries. These new measures will provide targeted relief for frequent drivers, farmers, and fishermen as economic pressure continues to mount.

events.title: 'Trump's high-stakes political theatre: US winning conventional war, Iran winning asymmetrical war'
events.summary: Delano D'Souza is pleased to welcome Dr. William Lawrence, Senior Academic and Research Fellow-in-Residence and the Director of the North African Area Studies Program at the National Council on US-Arab Relations. As a former US diplomat in the Muslim world, he views what we are witnessing as a deliberate embrace of chaos and unpredictability as a coercive tool. Yet such theatrical instability carries diminishing returns when used as a modus operandi, explains Dr. Lawrence. "Trump thinks he can force Iran to the table by being hyperbolic, apocalyptic and chaotic. Iranians, in turn, respond with so much mockery and bravado."

events.title: 🔴Second French peacekeeper dies after Saturday's ambush in Lebanon, Macron says
Residents pay the price as Afghanistan-Pakistan border remains closed
events.summary: The border between Pakistan and Afghanistan has been completely closed for six months, a direct consequence of the conflict between Kabul and Islamabad. Despite several mediation attempts, negotiations have failed, and the two countries remain at a diplomatic impasse in a war that has already displaced tens of thousands of people and claimed numerous lives.

events.title: Iran: 'Going back to war is not in anyone's interest,' risk analyst says
events.summary: A risk management analyst has spoken to FRANCE 24 about what it means, amid the ceasefire, if the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed. Paymon Azmoudeh is a senior consultant with the risk and compliance practice at Forward Global. He says he thinks the calculation on all sides is that going back to war is not in anyone's interest. He explained to us in Perspective what it means if the status quo continues with the ceasefire largely holding, but the economic impasse ongoing.

events.title: UK Starmer says unaware of security lapse in Mandelson security vetting
events.summary: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer was grilled during questions in Parliament over his decision to appoint Peter Mandelson as Britain's top diplomat to Washington, effectively brushing aside security concerns. Starmer once again faced calls to resign from the opposition, after former top civil servant Olly Robbins testified this week that there was an "atmosphere of pressure" from Downing Street to approve Mandelson's appointment. FRANCE 24's Bénédicte Paviot reports from London.

events.title: Lilies of the valley: Flowers given on International Workers' Day in France start to bloom
events.summary: It is a French tradition to give friends and family lilies of the valley on Labour day, the 1st of May. And after a rainy winter, these spring flowers have come into their own earlier than usual. Our colleagues at France 2 took a look behind the scenes at lily of the valley harvest.

events.title: REPLAY: UK Starmer takes questions in Parliament over Mandelson appointment
events.summary: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer was grilled during questions in Parliament over his decision to appoint Peter Mandelson as Britain's top diplomat to Washington, effectively brushing aside security concerns. Starmer faced calls to resign from the opposition, after former top civil servant Olly Robbins testified this week that there was an "atmosphere of pressure" from Downing Street to approve Mandelson's appointment so he could be in the post at the start of Donald Trump's second term in office.

events.title: EU resumes aid to Ethiopia suspended over Tigray war
events.summary: The European Union has said it will resume budgetary aid to Ethiopia, which was suspended when a brutal civil war broke out in 2020. The EU suspended its annual budgetary support payment in December 2020, a month after the start of the war between government forces and rebels from the northern Tigray region, which continued for two years and claimed some 600,000 lives. FRANCE 24's Tom Canetti reports from Addis Ababa, Ethiopia.

events.title: Nigeria faces jet fuel shortage as Strait of Hormuz disruption bites
events.summary: Nigeria’s jet fuel shortage is being driven by disruptions at the Strait of Hormuz that have curtailed global supplies of middle distillates, a fuel marketers’ group said, squeezing airlines already grappling with soaring costs. Products such as jet fuel and diesel that normally pass through the strategic waterway have become less available worldwide, pushing up prices, the Major Energies Marketers Association of Nigeria (MEMAN) said.

events.title: Cheri Cherin: Figure of the Congolese popular art movement honoured in Paris
events.summary: In Paris, a powerful tribute celebrates one of the most influential figures in Congolese painting. Chéri Chérin, who passed away just a few months ago, leaves behind a vibrant and socially engaged body of work. A voice of the people and a keen observer of his time, he played a key role in bringing Congolese popular art to an international audience. FRANCE 24’s Awa Diouf and Anaïs Chesnel report.

events.title: Zimbabwe: 8000 schools receive internet under Presidential Scheme
events.summary: In Zimbabwe, around 8000 schools are in the process of receiving internet access under the Presidential Internet Scheme. Thousands of antennas are to be installed in rural areas. The project, which started at the beginning of the year, could transform the lives of children, many of whom have never seen or used a computer linked to the internet.

events.title: EU gives preliminary approval to unblock €90 billion Ukraine loan
events.summary: EU member states on Wednesday gave a preliminary approval to unlock a €90 billion loan for Ukraine. The decision comes after delays linked to an oil dispute with Hungary over the Druzhba pipeline.

events.title: Iranian economy: Layoffs, inflation add to sanctions pain amid war
events.summary: The ongoing conflict between the US and Israel has created a profound economic crisis in Iran. As the government deals with the crippling internet blackouts and soaring inflation, a sense of uncertainty has taken hold of the nation's workforce.

events.title: In south Lebanon village, Hezbollah buries fighters killed in war with Israel
events.summary: Hezbollah held a mass funeral in south Lebanon's Kfar Sir on Tuesday for more than a dozen fighters killed during more than six weeks of war with Israel. The ceremony came after Israel and Lebanon entered into a 10-day ceasefire on Friday announced by US President Donald Trump. Iran-backed Hezbollah has not provided the total number of its fighters killed since Israel last month launched a massive wave of strikes and a ground invasion of Lebanon.

events.title: Ukraine to restart oil flows via Druzhba pipeline, potentially unblocking EU's loan
events.summary: Ukraine ​will resume pumping oil through the Druzhba pipeline on Wednesday, an industry source said, after President Volodymyr Zelenskiy said repairs were complete and urged the EU to unblock a 90-billion-euro loan halted by an argument over the pipe. A lengthy suspension of oil flows through the pipeline following a Russian attack on a pumping facility in Ukraine in January ​sparked an angry backlash ‌from European Union members Hungary and Slovakia, which remain reliant on Russian oil imports via Druzhba.

events.title: Devastated Gaza will need over $70 billion to rebuild, international report warns
events.summary: Gaza will need over seventy billion dollars in the next decade to rebuild from more than two years of devastating conflict. That's from a report this week from the UN, the EU and the World Bank, which has looked at the damage done after Israel went to war with Hamas in the Palestinian enclave following its attack on October 7th 2023.

events.title: Peru's electoral commission chief resigns over chaotic vote
events.summary: The head of Peru's electoral body resigned from his post Tuesday, hours before he was scheduled to be questioned by prosecutors over the chaotic presidential vote earlier this month. Peruvians went to the polls on April 12 to choose their ninth president in a decade, with the election dominated by voter anger at a political establishment seen as corrupt. The election day was marred by major logistical problems in the capital Lima, which prevented tens of thousands of people from voting until the following day.

events.title: How does the war in Iran end?
events.summary: 👉Listen to The World with Richard Engel and Yalda Hakim on your podcast app👈

events.title: Pope warns humanity's at risk, in new plea to world leaders
events.summary: The Pope has warned humanity's future risks being "tragically compromised" due to a breakdown of international law and political responsibility.

events.title: The tactic monkeys have adopted to settle stomachs after eating junk food
events.summary: Monkeys in Gibraltar have learned to eat soil to settle their stomachs from all the junk food they consume, research suggests.

events.title: Owner of gun used to kill eight children says it was stolen from his truck in weeks prior
events.summary: The owner of a gun which was used to kill eight children told investigators the firearm was stolen from his truck in the weeks before the shooting.

events.title: Mogul behind K-pop supergroup faces arrest
events.summary: South Korean police are seeking to arrest the music mogul behind K-pop supergroup BTS over an alleged £100m investor fraud scheme.

events.title: Two IDF soldiers jailed over smashing of Jesus statue
events.summary: Two Israeli soldiers have been jailed for 30 days and removed from combat duty after a statue of Jesus was destroyed.

events.title: Hundreds of gang members in mass trial over more than 47,000 crimes
events.summary: Hundreds of MS-13 gang members accused of homicides, drug and arms trafficking, and disappearances are the subject of a mass trial in El Salvador.

events.title: Airline cuts 20,000 flights
events.summary: Lufthansa has said 20,000 short-haul flights are being cut from its schedule this summer, allowing it to save on the soaring cost of jet fuel.

events.title: Holidays 'very likely' at risk as EU warns of 'crisis'
events.summary: The EU has warned that Europe is in a "very serious crisis" as aviation fuel stocks start to run low due to the war in Iran and people might have to change holiday plans this summer.

events.title: We would arrest Netanyahu, says Hungary's incoming PM
events.summary: Hungary's incoming prime minister has said he'll stop the country's withdrawal from the International Criminal Court (ICC).

events.title: Some who fled abuses in Equatorial Guinea fear pope’s visit might legitimize longtime ruler
events.summary: People who have fled abuses in Equatorial Guinea and are taking refuge elsewhere fear that the pope's visit to the Central African country could launder the image of Africa's longest-serving ruler

events.title: Alleged coup plotters in Nigeria arraigned on charges of treason and terrorism
events.summary: Six people accused of plotting to overthrow Nigerian President Bola Tinubu have been arraigned in court on charges of treason and terrorism

events.title: Japanese police arrest a South Korean for allegedly obstructing Yasukuni Shrine festival
events.summary: A South Korean national has been arrested in Tokyo for allegedly obstructing a spring festival at Yasukuni Shrine

events.title: Macron says French peacekeeper wounded in Lebanon attack over the weekend has died
events.summary: Macron says French peacekeeper wounded in Lebanon attack over the weekend has died

events.title: China's foreign and defense ministers meet with Cambodian counterparts in joint '2+2' dialogue
events.summary: Cambodia and China have held their first meeting of what is called a “2+2” Strategic Dialogue Mechanism

events.title: Sweden's secondhand clothing swaps offer a trendy way to cut environmental waste
events.summary: Thousands of people have attended Sweden’s annual clothing swap events across the Scandinavian country

events.title: Russia says its Africa Corps has freed Russian and Ukrainian citizens abducted in Niger
events.summary: Russia's Africa Corps has freed a Russian citizen and a Ukrainian abducted by an al-Qaida affiliate in Niger

events.title: Ukraine wants a Zelenskyy-Putin summit to jolt stalled US-led peace efforts
events.summary: Kyiv’s top diplomat says Ukraine is pushing for direct talks between President Zelenskyy and Russian President Putin

events.title: EU close to approving release of $106B loan to Ukraine after months of deadlock
events.summary: European Union envoys are close to approving a 90 billion euro ($106 billion) loan package to support Ukraine’s military and financial needs for the next two years

events.title: UK inflation rises as prices at the pump spike after Iran war
events.summary: Inflation in the U.K. climbed in March, driven by a sharp rise in fuel prices following the Iran war

events.title: Pope calls for justice and closing income gap in Equatorial Guinea Mass with presidential family
events.summary: Pope Leo XIV is urging Equatorial Guineans to work for freedom, justice and to close the gap “between the privileged and the disadvantaged.”

events.title: Americans injured in deadly Mexico pyramid shooting
events.summary: There are new details tonight in that deadly shooting rampage at a tourism hot spot in Mexico. Officials confirm six Americans were among the injured. NBC News’ Priscilla Thompson has the latest on the victims and what we’re learning about the shooter.

events.title: Beirut residents say they hope for peace amid tenuous ceasefire with Israel
events.summary: People in Beirut tell NBC News that they hope for permanent peace as a tenuous 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon holds, with the U.S. attempting to broker successful talks between the two countries.

events.title: Robot breaks the human half-marathon world record, a year after humanoid humiliation
events.summary: It was not even close as a bright-red Chinese humanoid named “Lightning” lived up to its name in a half-marathon pitting humans against robots, smoking its competition on Sunday.

events.title: Her 18-minute manifesto critiquing Putin went viral. Now others are speaking up.
events.summary: Blogger Victoria Bonya directly addressed Russian President Vladimir Putin, saying people were too afraid of him to speak the truth about the real state of affairs in Russia.

events.title: He was detained during Ramadan. Eight days later, his family collected his body.
events.summary: Crackdowns in Persian Gulf countries have chilled speech and limited the open-source evidence available to investigators tracking the conflict.

events.title: Trump may send Afghan allies who were promised new lives in the U.S. to Congo instead
events.summary: Hundreds of Afghan refugees who helped the United States fight the Taliban may be sent to the Democratic Republic of Congo after the Trump administration halted their U.S. resettlement, an advocacy group said

events.title: El Salvador holds mass trial for 486 alleged gang members
events.summary: A Salvadoran court on Tuesday began a collective trial of 486 alleged gang members, one of the biggest mass trials under President Nayib Bukele’s crackdown on gang violence through controversial emergency powers

events.title: Tourists describe terrifying moments during deadly shooting at Mexico pyramid site
events.summary: A visit to the world-famous Teotihuacán site devolved into chaotic moments as tourists jumped from atop the pyramids and tried to duck amid the spray of gunfire.

events.title: Gunman at Mexico's Teotihuacán pyramids kills Canadian woman, injures others
events.summary: A man fatally shot a ‌Canadian woman and injured several others ​before killing himself ​Monday at Mexico’s Teotihuacán ⁠pyramids, according to authorities.

events.title: Outrage after Israeli soldier seen striking Jesus statue in Lebanon
events.summary: A photograph showing an Israeli soldier damaging a statue of Jesus Christ has sparked public outrage. NBC News’ Molly Hunter reports on the Israeli military’s response to the incident.

events.title: Oil prices jump, adding to weeks of swings over Strait of Hormuz
events.summary: The price of oil jumped and stocks fell slightly Monday as traders digested renewed tensions between the U.S. and Iran over the critical Strait of Hormuz.U.S.

events.title: Israeli soldier seen striking Jesus statue in Lebanon, sparking condemnation
events.summary: Israel's leaders and military have condemned an Israeli soldier pictured damaging a statue of Jesus Christ

events.title: U.K. probes shadowy Islamic group amid arson spree at Jewish sites
events.summary: After a spate of arson attacks targeting Jewish sites in London, British authorities are investigating a shadowy online group with possible ties to Iran.

events.title: Trump says U.S. seized Iranian ship as tensions rise amid ceasefire
events.summary: The president accused Tehran of violating the truce and threatened strikes, while Iran warned it would retaliate, casting doubt on the timing of new talks.

events.title: Lebanese people displaced by Israeli strikes return to ruin
events.summary: “My house is demolished.”Imad Komeyha and his family spent 12 hours traveling from northern Lebanon back to Kfar Sir, a village in the south, only to find their house in ruins, laid to waste amid Israeli airstrikes

events.title: Hungary's new leader relishes dismantling Viktor Orbán's authoritarian state
events.summary: He's ousted the man who ruled for 16 years.

events.title: After war of words on Iran, Pope Leo says he's not interested in a debate with Trump
events.summary: Pope Leo XIV said it is not in his interest to debate President Donald Trump, pushing back at reporting about the escalating war of words between the two men

events.title: U.S. sanctions two firms over allegedly recruiting Colombian mercenaries in Sudan war
events.summary: CAIRO — The U.S. imposed sanctions on three people and two firms over allegedly recruiting and deploying Colombian mercenaries to fight alongside the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces in Sudan's war, which has entered its fourth year with no end in sight

events.title: Starmer's Mandelson nightmare never ends. This time, it might cost him his job.
events.summary: LONDON — British Prime Minister Keir Starmer probably wishes he had never heard the name Peter Mandelson

events.title: Oil plunges to lowest price in weeks after Iran says it will fully open Strait of Hormuz
events.summary: Oil prices plunged and two U.S. stock indexes hit all-time highs after Iran's foreign minister said the Strait of Hormuz was open for commercial vessels.
